titleStmt.title: A transcription of Romans in 1798
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Epistle to the Romans
Romans

respStmt.name: The International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: This transcription was made as part of the CATENA project, which has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no. 770816).

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

editionStmt.edition: Version 1.1, created on 21.05.2021, archived on the University of Birmingham eData repository on 13.5.2024.

publisher.name: The International Greek New Testament Project (IGNTP)

availability.p: Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) Available for re-use under a creative commons license provided attribution is made to the original creators.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription was produced using the online transcription editor developed by the Center for Digital Humanities at the University of Trier as part of the Workspace for Collaborative Editing Project between 2010 and 2013 and made available for use by the Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing and the University of Birmingham.
The XML specifications used are version 1.6 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

ab.note: An inscription can be found before the hypothesis on page 1.
An unknown number of pages are missing at this point.
Romans 2:10-15 have been misbound in the codex.
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Some words from the commentary are written as biblical text here (δοξα φησιν και τιμη και ειρηνη).
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Some biblical text is repeated here (ων το κατακριμα ενδικον εστι), which was possibly supposed to be commentary text.
Rom 4:4-12 have been misbound in the codex.
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Some commentary text is written as biblical text here (ει δε τις πνευμα χριστου ουκ εχει ουτος ουκ εστιν αυτου).
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
A section of commentary was written as biblical text and was subsequently deleted (ουκουν ουδε εφ ημων των εθνικων και των ιουδαιων αδικος ο θεος).
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
The commentary text has been written as part of the biblical text here (ερωτας ποθεν οι ισραηλιται απετυχον του δικαιωθηναι καιτοι σπουδαζοντες πολλα).
Untranscribed commentary text within the line
One line of untranscribed commentary text
One line of untranscribed commentary text
One line of untranscribed commentary text
Untranscribed commentary text within the line

Some commentary text was written as biblical text here (παλιν ετερα αντιθεσις ει εκηρυξαν μεν οι αποστολοι ουτοι δε ου συνηκαν αρα ουκ αξιοι συγγνωμης ειτα επαγει και τουτου την λυσιν). This was subsequently deleted.
The subscription is written at the bottom of the page below the commentary text.
